title: Traffic shifts need guardrails
description: Canary or percentage rollout should have baseline metrics, stop conditions, and a rollback owner before traffic increases.
body: Use for routing, deployment strategy, load balancer, service mesh, or cohort rollout changes. Do not recommend automated increase without evidence. Actions: define metrics such as error rate, latency, saturation, business success, and logs; set stop conditions. Validation signals include pre/post comparison and no alert regression. Risk boundary: missing observability blocks rollout advice. Use when Production Change Risk Controls work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
